Computer and Network Security

Spring 2026 - College Station

End Date: 05/05/2026

Wenhui Chu

Email:  chuw1@tamu.edu

Office Location:  ECRB 309B

Phone:  409-740-5516

Office Hours

TR:2:00pm-3pm

Catalog Description

Computer and Network Security. (3-0). Credit 3. Fundamental concepts and

principles of computer security, operating system and network security, secret key

and public key cryptographic algorithms, hash functions, authentication, firewalls

and intrusion detection systems, IPSec and VPN, wireless and web security.

Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in CSCE 315 or CSCE 331, and CSCE 313 ; junior

or senior classification; approval of instructor.

Course Prerequisites

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s):  Grade of C or better in CSCE 315 or CSCE 331, and

CSCE 313 ; junior or senior classification; approval of instructor.

Course Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

analyze a computing system to determine what security goals are desired

devise a strategy for meeting these goals

select appropriate cryptographic and security primitives

critique the security of software and network protocols

Special Course Designation

None

Textbook and/or Resource Materials

This material Is: Required

Introduction to Computer Security

ISBN:  0-321-51294-4

Authors:  Michael Goodrich, Roberto Tamassia

Publisher:  Addison-Wesley Pearson

This material Is: Recommended

Handbook of Applied Cryptography

URL for Resource:  http://cacr.uwaterloo.ca/hac/

Exams

Once you have taken the exams, you are not allowed to talk about its composition

with anyone. Any unauthorized sharing constitutes academic misconduct and will

be reported to the honors council and result in the application of an appropriate

sanction.

Assignments

Homework must be submitted electronically using Canvas, and will be due 11:59

p.m. of the due date, unless otherwise noted. Messages in the Canvas messaging

system, including comments attached to assignments, are not likely to be received

in a timely manner.

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

AI Statement

With the rise of artificial intelligence (AI) technologies, programming and problem-

solving practices are evolving. While these tools can assist with brainstorming,

debugging, or exploring alternative approaches, they  do not replace the essential

skills of designing, writing, and testing your own code . Nor do they substitute

for the critical thinking required to analyze problems, evaluate solutions, and apply

course concepts independently.

In this course, you are permitted to use generative AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT, GitHub

Copilot, Google Gemini) to support your learning, under the following conditions:

AI may be used as a supplement, not a substitute.  You may consult AI for

hints, debugging suggestions, or explanations, but you are expected to write

and understand your own code.

You must cite AI assistance.  If AI tools inform your work (e.g., generating a

code snippet, suggesting an algorithm, or helping you debug), you must

clearly document this in comments within your code (e.g.,  # AI tool

(ChatGPT) suggested this loop structure   ).

AI should not complete your assignments for you.  Submitting work

primarily written by AI is considered academic dishonesty. You are

responsible for understanding and being able to explain every line of code

you submit.

Limit reliance.  No more than 30% of an assignment's code may be directly

AI-generated. The majority of the work must reflect your own effort and

understanding.

Important reminders:

Generative AI may produce incorrect or inefficient code. Always test and

verify any AI-assisted solution before using it.

AI tools may generate code that does not follow best practices or course

requirements. You are responsible for ensuring your work meets assignment

expectations.

Misrepresentation of AI-generated work as your own will be reported to the

Aggie Honor System Office . The minimum penalties include:

A grade of 0 on the assignment

A grade of F in the course

Honor Violation Probation
